svg.text: Automatic service charge for large parties. Legally distinct from voluntary tip — configurable per revenue center.
Auto-gratuity is a SERVICE CHARGE (mandatory), not a voluntary TIP.
Under IRS Rev. Rul. 2012-18, mandatory service charges are restaurant revenue — servers receive them as WAGES (subject to withholding), not tips.
Voluntary tips are employee income reported by employee. Configure carefully.
Below threshold: voluntary tip shown · At or above: auto-grat applied
Manager PIN required + reason required for waiver
Auto Gratuity — Included for parties of 6+
⚠ Some jurisdictions tax mandatory service charges. Verify with accountant.
"An automatic gratuity of 18% will be added to parties of 6 or more."
How it looks on the register
Voluntary tip field is HIDDEN for this check (party of 7)
ⓘ An automatic gratuity of 18% is included for parties of 6 or more.
A mandatory gratuity of 18% has been
applied to your check for parties of 6+.
Thank you for dining with us!
Guest Request / Dissatisfied / VIP / Error / Other
Voluntary tip prompt shown after waiver
Stored: pos_payments.auto_grat_cents (separate from tip_cents). Tax: if taxable=true, auto_grat added to taxable base before tax calc.
Payroll: pos_auto_grat_distributions → Gusto export. Report: Tip Report shows auto-grat vs voluntary tip separately. IRS: auto-grat as wages on W-2, not tip income.
